subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Shivering and Vasoconstriction
description: A quick win: understand Shivering and Vasoconstriction.

hook.text: Shivering generates heat by making your muscles contract rapidly—up to 500 watts of emergency warmth.

buildup.text: Before shivering starts, blood vessels in your skin constrict to keep warm blood near your core.

discovery.text: This vasoconstriction is why your fingers turn white in cold—blood retreats from extremities.

twist.text: When shivering stops in extreme cold, it's not improvement—the body has exhausted its energy.

climax.text: Stopped shivering in a cold person means severe hypothermia and a life-threatening emergency.

punchline.text: Shivering is a survival engine. When it stops in cold, it's failed.

quiz.question: What does it mean when a cold person stops shivering?
options: Severe hypothermia—the body has exhausted its energy
They have adapted to the cold
